content: Hey, I just adopted a new dog named Willow from a shelter. She's a golden retriever and she loves going on walks with me in the park near my home in Paris.
Congratulations on the new furry family member! How's Willow adjusting to her new home? Does she have any favorite toys or treats?
She loves playing with her stuffed rabbit and going on long walks. I've also started taking her to the gardens near the opera house where I perform. It's great exercise for both of us.
That sounds wonderful. As an opera singer, do you find that your performances are affected by your new role as a dog owner? Do you have to travel often for work?

Yes, I do travel quite a bit, but I've been lucky to have a great dog sitter who takes care of Willow when I'm away. I'm actually performing in La Bohème at the Palais Garnier next month, and then I'll be heading to New York for a few weeks.
La Bohème is a classic. I'm sure you'll do great. How do you like to spend your free time when you're not performing or taking care of Willow?

I love gardening and trying out new recipes in the kitchen. I've recently gotten into cooking Thai food, and I find it really relaxing. I also enjoy doing crossword puzzles and playing chess in my spare time.
Thai food is delicious. Have you tried growing any of the ingredients you use in your cooking, like lemongrass or basil, in your garden?
Actually, yes. I have a small herb garden on my balcony, and I grow all sorts of things like rosemary, thyme, and mint. It's so nice to be able to just step outside and pick what I need.
That sounds amazing. I'm sure Willow enjoys exploring the balcony with you. Does she have a favorite spot to lounge in the sun?

She loves lying in the sunbeams that come through the windows, and she's also really fond of chasing after balls in the park. I've been thinking of taking her on a trip to the countryside soon, maybe to go mushroom foraging.
Mushroom foraging sounds like a fun adventure. Are you planning on going to a specific location, like the forests near Fontainebleau?

Yes, that's exactly where I was thinking of going. I've heard the forests there are full of all sorts of interesting mushrooms, and it would be a great opportunity for Willow to run around and get some exercise.
I'm sure you'll have a great time. Don't forget to bring some water and snacks for Willow, and maybe even a map so you don't get lost.
Will do, thanks for the advice. I'm really looking forward to spending some quality time with Willow and enjoying the beautiful scenery.
You're welcome. I hope you and Willow have a wonderful time on your adventure. Don't hesitate to reach out if you need any more tips or recommendations.
Thanks, I will. I should probably get going now, I have a rehearsal to get to. Nice chatting with you!
You too, Finley. Break a leg at your rehearsal, and I hope to talk to you again soon.
